title: 5 Files Outgoing from CentralВ 1
body_text:
5	Files Outgoing from CentralВ 1
5.1	Overview of Files Outgoing from CentralВ 1
Outgoing files are created by CentralВ 1 and posted to the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs folder on the File and Report Exchange. Financial institutions and banking system providers download the files from CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange for further processing (e.g., reconciliation).
5.2	AFT Clearing File Specifications
The purpose of the AFT clearing files is to post transactions to the membersГўВҖВҷ accounts. This includes all transactions that relate to direct deposit and preГўВҖВ“authorized debit transactions originated either by the financial institution (for a financial institution member or business member) or by another financial institution for delivery to the financial institution (for a financial institution member or business member). For details on CAD and USD AFT clearing file specifications, refer to the following:
AFT Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD (BC and Atlantic Region),
Form 6269
AFT Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ USD (BC and Atlantic Region),
Form 6270
AFT Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD (All Regions Except BC and Atlantic),
Form 9083
AFT Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ USD (All Regions Except BC and Atlantic),
Form 9084
AFT Return Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD (All Regions Except BC and Atlantic),
Form 9085
AFT Return Clearing File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ USD (All Regions Except BC and Atlantic),
Form 9086
5.3	Qualified Debit Clearing Items
The purpose of the debit clearing files is to post Canadian and US dollar cheques drawn on a financial institution or financial institution customer and managed through automated processes. See the Incoming Clearing Items Part for more information. File specifications for Canadian and US dollar qualified debit clearing items are available as follows:
DREC Qualified Debit Clearing Items File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD Cheques (BC Only),
Form 6271
QREC Qualified Debit Clearing Items File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ USD Cheques (BC Only),
Form 6272
XDREC Qualified Debit Clearing Items File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD Cheques,
Form 9319
XQREC Qualified Debit Clearing Items File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ USD Cheques,
Form 9320
DCPA and QCPA Qualified Debit Clearing Items File Specifications ГўВҖВ“ CAD and USD Cheques (All Regions Except BC and Atlantic),
Form 9528
5.4	PREC File Specifications (BC Only)
The PREC file contains all transactions posted to the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs CentralВ 1 account on a specific date. This file can help financial institutions automatically reconcile their Canadian dollar, US dollar, Sterling, and Euro CentralВ 1 accounts. The PREC file and the Account Reconciliation service are intended for multi-branch financial institutions and are available on a subscription basis. The financial institution develops an automated match program to compare its banking systemГўВҖВҷs data to CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs data. The financial institution is responsible for creating its own match program.
For details on file availability, structure, and format, see PREC File Specifications (BC Only),
Form 3750
.
5.5	OREC File Specifications
The OREC file contains all transactions posted to the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs CentralВ 1 account on a specific date. This file can help financial institutions automatically reconcile their Canadian dollar, US dollar, Sterling, and Euro CentralВ 1 accounts. The OREC file and Account Reconciliation service are intended for multi-branch financial institutions and are available on a subscription basis. The financial institution develops an automated match program to compare its banking systemГўВҖВҷs data to CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs data. The financial institution is responsible for creating its own match program.
For details on file availability, structure, and format see OREC File Specifications,
Form 8951
.
Note: 	Financial institutions that currently subscribe to the PREC file will automatically receive both the PREC and the OREC file. No additional subscription is necessary. For new subscriptions, only the OREC file will be provided as OREC encompasses all of the same information as PREC and includes the additional fields: Reference ID, Description, Processing Date and Opening & Closing balances.
5.6	Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI and Flat File Specifications
The Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data files contain payments made to Corporate Creditors from participating financial institutions. These payments are made through electronic payment channels.
For details on the Flat file structure and format, see Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ Flat File Specifications,
Form 2665
.
For details on the EDI file structure and format, see Bill Payment Remittance Processing Data Files ГўВҖВ“ EDI File Specifications,
Form 2667
.
5.7	HREC File Specifications
The HREC file is used for reconciliation purposes and is available to subscribing financial institutions and centrals. The file lists the bill payment transactions received from different channels that have been processed through CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs Bill Payment System. The file is generated daily and can be used to calculate totals for settlement or for bill payment reconciliation processes against the records on the bank host system.
For details on file structure and format, see HREC File Specifications,
Form 6118
.
5.8	EMTC and EMFC File Specifications
The EMTC and EMFC files
are available to financial institutions and centrals to assist with the reconciliation of
Interac
e-Transfer
ГӮВ®
transactions.
The EMTC file contains the following:
Pending
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that are currently outstanding
Interac
e-Transfer transactions canceled by the sender since the last settlement
Interac
e-Transfer transactions canceled by the system since the last settlement
Interac
e-Transfer transactions that have been completed since the last settlement
The EMFC is an optional file that contains the same details as the EMTC, but it also includes the details of all failed
Interac
e-Transfer transactions. Changes may be required to the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs banking system to use the EMFC file.
For details on file structure and format, see EMTC/EMFC File Specifications,
Form 6195
.
5.9	Deposit File Specifications
This document describes the specifications for files that contain deposits captured by CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs Mobile Remote Deposit Capture service. This file is provided to financial institutions to reconcile deposits recorded by their banking systems to those settled by CentralВ 1 through the item processing system. For details on file structure and format, see Deposit File Specifications,
Form 3604
.
5.10	Corporate Capture XML Deposit File Specifications
The
purpose of this document is to describe the requirements for the Corporate Capture XML Deposit File. The deposit file is provided to financial institutions to reconcile deposits recorded by their banking systems through Real Time Engine (RTE) messages to those settled by CentralВ 1 through the item processing system. If your financial institution hasnГўВҖВҷt implemented RTE, the file can be used to post credits to your membersГўВҖВҷ accounts through an automated or manual process.
For details on file structure and format, see Corporate Capture XML Deposit File Specifications,
Form 9349
.
5.11	Wires Data Extract Service File Specifications
The Wires Data Extract Service provides subscribing financial institutions with an XML data file and a report file for FINTRAC reporting purposes. Financial institutions import the data file to an AML system or send the file to an AML service provider. The information in the file is used to determine which incoming and outgoing wires are reported to FINTRAC.
Depending on your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs requirements, the files include
all incoming and outgoing wires, or
only cross-border incoming and outgoing wires.
For details on file structure and format, see Wires Data Extract Services File Specifications,
Form 9327
.
END
